questions.transcribedStem: Eugene used the set-up below to find out the time taken for a bob to make one swing. The bob makes one swing when it moves from A to B and back to A.
Unripe bananas are green and contain chlorophyll.
questions.answer: (a) The bananas grow upwards to receive as much sunlight as possible to make food/carry out photosynthesis. | Explanation: - Observe from the diagram that the unripe bananas contain chlorophyll, which is the green pigment that absorbs light energy for making food. | (b) Experimental set-up: One unripe banana and one ripe banana | Control set-up: One unripe banana | Explanation: - Placing one unripe banana and one ripe banana in the experimental set-up and only one unripe banana in the control set-up allows us to compare the difference in the time taken for the unripe banana to ripen. This difference is only due to the presence of the ripe banana in the experimental set-up as all other variables are kept the same.

questions.transcribedStem: The adult of bird K lays its egg in the nest of another type of bird, L. This only happens when the adult of bird L is not at its nest. Bird K flies away after laying its egg and does not return.
questions.answer: (a) The adult of bird L is unable to distinguish the egg of bird K from its own eggs due to their similar appearances. Hence, it will take care and help to incubate the egg of bird K together with its own eggs, increasing the chance of hatching of the egg of bird K. | (b) The population size of bird L will decrease because many young of bird L will die as they are unable to survive the fall from the nest or due to the lack of food. | Explanation: - The diagram shows that the adult of bird L is feeding the young, hence if the young of bird L is pushed out of the nest by the young of bird K, the young of bird L may face a lack of food. | (c) The population size of bird K will decrease because adults of bird L can look out for their neighbours' nests, making it more difficult for adults of bird K to lay their eggs in the nests of bird L without being seen. | Explanation: - The information 'This only happens when the adult of bird L is not at its nest' tells us that the adult of bird K lays its egg in the nest of bird L when not being watched.

questions.transcribedStem: A farmer measured the amount of light entering his greenhouse at four different times of the day.
questions.answer: (a) The rate of photosynthesis is the highest at 12 pm because the plants receive the greatest amount of light to make food. | Explanation: - Photosynthesis takes place in the presence of light. The higher the amount of light available for the plants, the higher the rate of photosynthesis. | (b) Air is a poor conductor of heat. On a cold day, the pockets of air slow down heat loss from inside the greenhouse to the cold surroundings outside the greenhouse, keeping the inside of the greenhouse warm. | Explanation: - Heat flows from a region of higher temperature to a region of lower temperature.

questions.syllabusTopic: Life cycles in plants and animals
questions.transcribedStem: Diagram 1 shows a process, R, that takes place in the life cycle of a coconut plant.
questions.answer: (a) Germination | (b) Air/Oxygen, water and warmth/suitable temperature | (c)(i) Fleshy solid P serves as a food source for the baby plant to grow before the baby plant can make its own food using structure J. | Explanation: - Structure J is the first leaf of the young plant. Before the first leaves are developed, the young plant is unable to make its own food through photosynthesis. Hence, the fleshy solid P is important in providing food for the baby plant. | (c)(ii) The air trapped in the fibrous husk / empty space of the coconut enables the coconut fruit to float on water and be carried by moving water away from the parent tree.

questions.transcribedStem: The Aedes mosquito spreads dengue fever in humans.
questions.answer: (a)(i) From March to August, as the amount of rainfall increases, the number of dengue cases also increases. In months with more rainfall, there is more still/stagnant water for Aedes mosquitoes to lay their eggs and the eggs to hatch into larvae and pupae which live in water, and subsequently grow into adults that can spread dengue fever. | Explanation: - Mosquitoes have a four-stage life cycle. During their egg, larval and pupal stages, they live in water. | (a)(ii) It takes one to two weeks for mosquitoes to become adults after the eggs are laid. | Explanation: - From the graphs, the amount of rainfall starts to increase in February while the number of dengue cases starts to increase in March, after about three to four weeks. This is about the sum of number of weeks for mosquitoes to become adults after the eggs are laid (one to two weeks) and number of weeks for dengue fever to develop after a person is bitten (about two weeks).

(b) As the female mosquitoes mated with male mosquitoes carrying bacteria W, they laid eggs that would not hatch. When fewer eggs hatched, fewer young were produced and grew into adults. Hence, there were fewer adult female Aedes mosquitoes to bite people and thus, the number of dengue cases decreased.

questions.transcribedStem: A farmer grew some young plants far apart from one another to prevent overcrowding.
questions.answer: (a) Plants that grow far apart do not have to compete with one another for sunlight, water and mineral salts/nutrients necessary for their healthy growth. | (b) These insects fed on the stems so as to feed on the food made by the plants that are transported through the food-carrying tubes in the stems. | (c)(i) The large ants can feed on the sweet sticky substance produced by the tiny insects. | (c)(ii) The large ants protect the tiny insects from their predators. | Explanation: - The tiny insects do not have wings and cannot move far. Hence, they could be vulnerable to predators.

questions.transcribedStem: Peter has a toy train that runs on batteries. He placed plates of food on the train as shown below.
questions.answer: (a) Battery (Chemical potential energy) -> Wires (Electrical energy) -> Train (Kinetic energy + Heat energy / Sound energy) | Explanation: - Heat energy and sound energy are generated due to friction between the mechanical parts in the train and between the wheels and track. | (b) When the food was removed, the train became lighter and hence, less electrical energy was converted into heat and sound due to friction and more electrical energy was converted into kinetic energy for the train to move faster. | Explanation: - The greater the kinetic energy the train possesses, the faster the train moves. | (c) Some electrical energy was converted into gravitational potential energy and hence, less electrical energy was converted into kinetic energy and the train moved more slowly. | Explanation: - H is positioned higher than G, hence there is more gravitational potential energy at H than at G.

(a) Temperature is a measure of the degree of hotness of an object. | Explanation: - Temperature and heat are different. - Heat is a form of energy. | (b) 20°C | Explanation: - Observe that the temperature of the hot water decreases. This is because it loses heat to the cooler surroundings. - The temperature of the water decreases until it reaches the same temperature as the surroundings. | (c) The temperature of the water changes rapidly during the first five minutes. | Explanation: - The rate of heat loss of the water in the beaker is higher when the difference in temperature of water and that of the surroundings is larger. - As the temperature of the water in the beaker decreases, the rate of heat loss to the surroundings decreases. Hence, in the first five minutes, the change in temperature of the hot water is rapid.

questions.transcribedStem: Seng Huat connected his phone to the electricity supply with a wire. The plug of the wire fitted the phone properly.
questions.answer: (a) Rubber is an electrical insulator. | Explanation: - An electrical insulator is a material that does not conduct electricity. It keeps us safe from the wires conducting electricity. | (b)(i) Friction between the plug and the phone holds the phone in place. | Explanation: - Friction between surfaces can prevent slipping. | (b)(ii) The weight of the phone is greater than the friction between the plug and the phone. | Explanation: - When two opposing forces act on an object, the object will remain stationary if the forces are equal. The object will move in the direction of the greater force if the forces are not equal.

questions.answer: (a) See answer image. Student needs to draw a line from point A of switch 1 to point A of switch 2.  Also straight line from point B of switch 1 to point B of switch 2.  Then student needs to draw a wire loop from left of switch 1 to right of switch 2.  In the loop there should be a battery, and the bulb is in the loop.  The wires connecting to the bulb should have one connecting at the metal tip, and the other at the side casing. | Explanation: - Observe from the table in the question that the circuit is closed only when both switches are at A or both switches are at B. This shows that position A of one switch and position B of the other switch are not connected. | (b) Add the new bulb in parallel to the first bulb. | Explanation: - In a parallel circuit, when each pathway contains only one bulb, the brightness of each bulb is the same. - When two bulbs are connected in series, the brightness of the bulb decreases as compared to a single bulb connected in series.

questions.transcribedStem: A worker used a truck to deliver blocks of ice. A mist was seen when ice was taken out of the truck as shown.
questions.answer: (a) The water vapour in the surrounding air lost heat to the cooler air that came out of the truck when the ice was taken out. The cooled water vapour condensed and formed fine water droplets as mist. | Explanation: - Heat travels from a region of higher temperature to a region of lower temperature. - When a gas loses sufficient heat to the surroundings, it condenses into a liquid. | (b) The mist took in heat from the warmer surroundings and evaporated to form colourless water vapour. | Explanation: - Mist forms when the water vapour loses heat and condenses into fine water droplets. - Mist disappears when the fine water droplets gain heat and evaporate. | (c) The plastic strips helped to keep the cold air inside the truck and prevented warmer air from the surroundings from entering the truck. Hence, the heat flow from the warmer surroundings to the ice to cause it to melt was reduced. | Explanation: - Ice melts as it gains heat from its surroundings. When the heat gained by the ice is reduced, the melting of ice is reduced.

questions.transcribedStem:
Samy set up a doorbell as shown.

The iron cylinder and electric circuit are fixed in place. The iron rod can swing about point P. A spring is fixed to the iron rod. The other end of the spring is fixed.

When Samy closed the switch, the iron rod hit the bell to produce a 'ding' sound.

When he opened the switch, the iron rod hit plate R to produce a 'dong' sound.
questions.answer: (a) When the switch is closed, an electric current flows through the coil of wire and magnetises the iron cylinder. The magnetised iron cylinder then attracts the iron rod. The iron rod swings about point P and the head of the iron rod hits the bell to produce a 'ding' sound. | Explanation: - The magnetic force exerted by the electromagnet overcomes the elastic spring force of the spring. | (b) Increase the number of turns of the coil of wire around the iron cylinder. Add more batteries in series to the circuit. | Explanation: - The number of turns of the coil of wire around the electromagnet core and the amount of current in the circuit affect the strength of the electromagnet. - When the magnetic force of the electromagnet is stronger, it produces a larger impact when the head of the iron rod hits the bell, hence producing a louder sound. | (c) Use a stiffer spring. The stiffer spring will exert a stronger pulling force on the iron rod when the switch is opened, hence striking plate R harder to produce a louder 'dong' sound. | Explanation: - A stiffer spring exerts a stronger elastic spring force.

Less light could penetrate the surface of the pond and less light was available for plant G to carry out the most photosynthesis, and produced the highest volume of oxygen. | (b) The population of bird A would increase. More plant F would be available as food to bird A. | (c) With fewer plant G, there is less food for bird B. questions.transcribedStem: In the circuit shown, P and Q are identical bulbs. All components are working. When switch S is closed, the iron bar moves up and down between points A and B, and bulb P turns on and off repeatedly.
questions.answer: (a) When switch S was closed, a closed circuit is formed and allows electric current to flow through bulbs P and Q and the fixed iron cylinder which becomes an electromagnet. Bulbs P and Q light up. The electromagnet attracts the iron bar which moves to point B and touches the metal contacts. This causes electric current to stop flowing through bulb P and the fixed iron cylinder. Bulb P no longer lights up. The fixed iron cylinder stops working as an electromagnet and no longer attracts the iron bar. The iron bar pulls back to point A, touches the metal contacts and closes the circuit. The process then repeats, and iron bar moves up and down between points A and B, and bulb P turns on and off repeatedly. | (b) Bulbs P and Q | (c) Bulb Q is brighter when iron bar is at B. Bulb Q receives current from the two batteries in the circuit. With more current passing through bulb Q, it lights up brighter.

questions.transcribedStem: Mariam set up the circuit in a toy boat as shown. The metal contacts, A and B, touched the magnet but are not fixed to it. The magnet is resting on a wooden support. When Mariam closed the switch, the plastic captain moved up and down continuously.
questions.answer: (a) Electric current flowed through the closed circuit when the switch was closed. The fixed iron cylinder became an electromagnet and repelled the magnet upwards with the plastic captain. Metal contact B lost contact with the magnet and this created an open circuit. Electric current would stop flowing through the wires, causing the fixed iron cylinder to lose its magnetic properties and hence, stop repelling the magnet. The magnet dropped back to its original position with the plastic captain. Metal contact B touched the magnet once again and a closed circuit would be formed with electric current flowing through the circuit once more. The whole process would be repeated. | (b) The captain moved up to a lower height than before. After playing for a few hours, a lot of the chemical potential energy in the batteries would be converted into electrical energy. Therefore, less chemical potential energy was available to be converted into less electrical energy which led to a weaker electromagnet formed in the iron cylinder, causing a weaker repulsion between the two magnets. The magnet with the plastic captain repelled away slower and lower than before. | Explanation: Batteries store a fixed amount of chemical potential energy which gets converted into electrical energy when required.
